events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen guitarist Brian May celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Brian May Birthday
angles.copy: The legendary Queen guitarist and astrophysicist Brian May is celebrating his 79th birthday this Sunday. What's your favourite riff from the man who brought us 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and so many more?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Mick Jagger Birthday
angles.copy: Still rocking stages worldwide, the iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Mick Jagger, marks his 83rd birthday this week. Which Stones track always gets you moving?

events.title: MTV Launched
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched in the United States, famously playing The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first music video.

angles.name: MTV Launch Anniversary
angles.copy: It's 45 years since MTV burst onto our screens, forever changing the music landscape. And yes, the first video was 'Video Killed The Radio Star' – ironic, isn't it?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: 32 years ago this week, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and kickstarted a new era of British rock. What's your go-to track from that iconic album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival Anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, took place from August 15 to 18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Woodstock Anniversary
angles.copy: 57 years ago, half a million people descended on a dairy farm in upstate New York for three days of peace, love, and music. The spirit of Woodstock lives on.

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: The music world remembers Elvis Presley, who passed away on this day in 1977, at the age of 42.

angles.name: Elvis Death Anniversary
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us 49 years ago. His music continues to inspire generations.

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: The music world remembers Tupac Shakur, who was fatally shot in Las Vegas in 1996, at the age of 25.

angles.name: Tupac Death Anniversary
angles.copy: 30 years ago, the music world lost an icon. We remember Tupac Shakur, a voice that defined a generation, who would have been 55 today.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: The music world remembers guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on this day in 1970, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Death Anniversary
angles.copy: On this day, we pay tribute to the unparalleled guitar virtuoso, Jimi Hendrix, who left us 56 years ago. His influence on music remains immeasurable.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', featuring the iconic zebra crossing cover, on September 26, 1969, in the UK and October 1, 1969, in the US.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road', an album instantly recognisable by that legendary cover. Which side is your favourite – the medley or the standalone tracks?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman Chrissie Hynde celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! The voice of The Pretenders, still rocking after all these years. What's your favourite Pretenders track?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Former Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Liam Gallagher Birthday
angles.copy: The one and only Liam Gallagher celebrates his 54th birthday today. Whether it's Oasis or his solo work, the man's got anthems. What's your top Liam track?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine
events.blurb: John Lennon released his iconic album 'Imagine', featuring the timeless title track, on September 9, 1971, in the US and October 8, 1971, in the UK.

angles.name: Imagine Album Anniversary
angles.copy: 55 years ago, John Lennon gave the world 'Imagine', a song that still resonates with its message of peace. A true masterpiece.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple introduced the first generation iPod on October 23, 2001, and it began shipping on November 10, 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: iPod Launch Anniversary
angles.copy: Remember the original iPod? 25 years ago, Apple dropped this game-changer, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. It completely changed how we consumed music.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: James Bond Day
angles.copy: It's James Bond Day! Celebrating the world's most famous secret agent, first seen on screen in 'Dr. No' back in '62. What's your favourite Bond theme tune?

events.title: Remembering John Lennon
events.blurb: The music world remembers John Lennon, who would have been 86 today.

angles.name: John Lennon Birthday Tribute
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the legendary John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire generations worldwide.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: Actor and comedian John Cleese, known for Monty Python and Fawlty Towers, celebrates his 87th birthday.

angles.name: John Cleese Birthday
angles.copy: The brilliant John Cleese celebrates his 87th birthday today. From Python to Fawlty Towers, his comedic genius is undeniable. What's your favourite Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album'
events.blurb: The Beatles released their ninth studio album, officially titled 'The Beatles' but commonly known as 'The White Album', on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: 58 years ago, The Beatles gave us the sprawling, diverse, and iconic 'White Album'. Which track from that double LP do you always go back to?
